mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the top-minus-bottom PAYEMS growth decile spread is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the absence of near-term monetary tightening risk compresses equity risk premiums and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales and declining unemployment captures consumer-driven momentum more completely than either variable in isolation, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) versus additive specifications, or the joint signal underperforms the best single proxy by more than 50% in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of recessions, accelerating industrial production signals capacity utilization normalization and operating leverage recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days; this effect vanishes in late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery regimes, INDPRO growth fails to predict 20-day return direction with accuracy above 55%, or the long-short portfolio spread is not positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales directly translate to higher near-term corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or month-over-month RSAFS increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 1.4 million units signal durable construction demand and positive employment spillovers that propagate to broad equity earnings, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following trigger activation are negative in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising month-over-month consumer prices while the federal funds rate is pinned near zero create negative real rate shocks that compress corporate margin forecasts and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation pressures
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels shows no significant negative coefficient (t-statistic above -1.5), or the joint signal fails to underperform the unconditional baseline in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of observations.
